User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the Secure Assessment Gateway, distinguishing between administrators and students. Authentication details are managed by an external system (Supabase Auth).

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for identification.

role.description: The role of the user within the system (e.g., 'admin', 'student').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

Exam.title: Exam
Exam.description: Defines a multiple-choice examination, including its structure, time limits, and passing criteria.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Exam entity.

description.description: A brief description or instructions for the exam.

timeLimitMinutes.description: The maximum time allowed to complete the exam, in minutes.

passingScore.description: The minimum score required to pass this exam.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User (admin) who created this exam. (Relationship: User 1:N Exam)

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a single multiple-choice question belonging to an exam, including its text, options, and the correct answer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

examId.description: Reference to the Exam this question belongs to. (Relationship: Exam 1:N Question)

questionText.description: The main text of the multiple-choice question.

options.description: An ordered list of possible answer options for the question.

correctOptionIndex.description: The zero-based index indicating which option in the 'options' array is the correct answer.

Result.title: Result
Result.description: Stores the outcome of a student's attempt at an exam, including their score, timestamps, and integrity status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Result entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the User (student) who took the exam. (Relationship: User 1:N Result)

examId.description: Reference to the Exam that was attempted. (Relationship: Exam 1:N Result)

score.description: The final score achieved by the student on the exam.

startedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the student started the exam.

completedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the student completed or submitted the exam.

integrityStatus.description: A status indicating any anti-cheat flags or suspicious activities detected during the exam (e.g., 'clean', 'flagged-tab-switch').

definition.description: Stores core profile information for each user (students), identified by their Supabase Auth UID. Admin roles are segregated into a separate collection for security.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (Supabase Auth UID) of the user.

definition.description: A marker collection for administrators. The existence of a document at this path signifies that the corresponding user (by UID) has admin privileges, enabling efficient, authorization-independent role checks without requiring a 'get()' call. Documents can be empty or contain minimal data like a timestamp.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier (Supabase Auth UID) of the administrator.

definition.description: Contains definitions for multiple-choice examinations created by administrators. Includes the 'createdBy' field, which identifies the admin owner for authorization.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exam.

definition.description: Stores individual questions for an exam. Includes denormalized 'examCreatedBy' (copied from the parent Exam's 'createdBy' field) for authorization independence. The 'correctOptionIndex' field is for admin access only; students access sanitized questions via RPCs.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent exam.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question.

definition.description: Stores the outcome and details of a student's attempt at an exam. This path ensures path-based ownership, allowing students to access only their own results. Admins can view all results via a collection group query.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier (Supabase Auth UID) of the student who took the exam.

params.name: resultId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exam result.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the Secure Assessment Gateway is designed with paramount focus on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Query As Protection (QAP) principles. This ensures robust security rules that are simple to write and debug, and prevents complex hierarchical dependencies.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   **User Roles (Admins):** Instead of checking a 'role' field on a user's document using `get()` (which breaks atomic operations), a dedicated top-level collection `/admin_roles/{adminId}` is used. The mere *existence* of a document at this path signifies an administrator. This allows for a `request.auth.uid in /admin_roles` check, which is a fast and independent authorization mechanism, fulfilling Authorization Independence.
*   **Exam Ownership for Questions:** For questions (`/exams/{examId}/questions/{questionId}`), authorization to modify a question depends on who created the parent exam. To avoid a `get('/exams/{examId}')` call in the rules, the `createdBy` field from the parent `Exam` document is denormalized and copied into each `Question` document as `examCreatedBy`. This allows security rules on `questions` to directly verify admin ownership (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.examCreatedBy`) without cross-document reads, thus ensuring Authorization Independence.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs:**
*   **User Data:** Core user profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}`. This path-based ownership naturally segregates each user's data, making rules like `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;` straightforward and ensuring QAPs are met (a user can only list their own specific data by providing their UID).
*   **Admin Roles:** The `/admin_roles/{adminId}` collection structurally segregates administrators from regular users, making admin checks (`isAdmin()`) explicit and performant via existence checks.
*   **Exams:** The `/exams/{examId}` collection holds exam definitions. It is made universally readable for authenticated users (`allow list: if request.auth != null;`) to allow students to browse available exams, while write access is restricted to the `createdBy` admin. This supports QAPs by allowing efficient listing of exams without exposing unauthorized information.
*   **Questions:** Questions are stored as a subcollection `/exams/{examId}/questions/{questionId}`. Crucially, the application requirements state, 'The client never receives correct answers or controls the authoritative clock.' This implies that `correctOptionIndex` should not be directly exposed to students via Firestore reads. Therefore, Firestore rules for this path are designed to primarily grant full read/write access *only* to the admin who created the exam (using the denormalized `examCreatedBy` field). Students will receive sanitized question data (without answers) through Supabase RPCs, which acts as a robust form of structural segregation for sensitive data.
*   **Results:** Student exam results are stored under `/users/{studentId}/results/{resultId}`. This path-based ownership ensures that students can only read and write their *own* results. For administrators, a collection group query on `results` combined with an `isAdmin()` check allows listing all results, fulfilling QAPs for both students (private results) and admins (oversight).
